title: Day 19: Iranian Cluster Missiles Kill Elderly Couple in Ramat Gan; Ali Larijani Confirmed Dead; USS Ford Ablaze
detail: Day 19 of Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion. Iranian cluster munitions killed a couple in their 70s in Ramat Gan — the first confirmed Israeli civilian fatalities from cluster warheads during the conflict. Eight suspected shrapnel impact sites were reported across greater Tel Aviv, including damage to Tel Aviv's Savidor Central Station. The IRGC explicitly claimed the overnight cluster missile barrage was 'in revenge for the killing of Larijani,' confirming Ali Larijani's assassination. IDF struck IRGC Security Unit headquarters, IRGC General Logistics and Support Department, and a ballistic missile array command center. US forces deployed 5,000-pound deep-penetrating bunker busters on hardened Iranian missile sites near the Strait of Hormuz. The USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier sustained a 30-hour onboard fire and is heading for repairs — a significant US naval capability degradation. Mojtaba Khamenei's status remains unverified; Israeli reports indicate he was wounded and that his mother, wife, and son were killed. Hormuz traffic showed slight increase, with Iran reportedly allowing more ships to transit. Brent crude fell to ~$101/bbl. Trump said the US is 'not ready to leave Iran yet' but will do so 'in the very near future.' Joe Kent, Trump's counterterrorism chief, resigned — the highest-level US official to leave over the Iran war.

title: Iranian Cluster Missiles Kill Elderly Couple in Ramat Gan — First Confirmed Israeli Cluster Fatalities
detail: Iranian ballistic missiles carrying cluster warheads struck the Tel Aviv suburb of Ramat Gan overnight, killing a couple in their 70s — the first confirmed Israeli civilian fatalities from cluster munitions in the conflict. Eight suspected shrapnel impact sites were identified across greater Tel Aviv. Fragments struck Tel Aviv's Savidor Central Station, Israel's main rail hub in the Tel Aviv area, causing damage and disrupting services. Two additional people sustained light shrapnel injuries. Israeli medics described facing a 'new battlefield' of unexploded cluster submunitions scattered across urban neighborhoods. The IRGC stated the barrage was 'in revenge for the killing of [Ali] Larijani.' Israel is not a signatory to the Convention on Cluster Munitions, and neither is Iran.

title: Ali Larijani Confirmed Killed — IRGC Cites His Death as Justification for Ramat Gan Cluster Strike
detail: The IRGC Aerospace Force explicitly stated that the overnight cluster missile barrage on Ramat Gan was 'in revenge for the killing of Larijani,' confirming Ali Larijani's assassination. Ali Larijani, Iran's National Security Council Secretary and one of the most powerful figures in the Islamic Republic, was killed in an earlier US-Israeli airstrike. His death eliminates one of the last senior figures with direct experience in diplomatic negotiations and removes a potential pragmatic voice from Iran's leadership. Netanyahu stated 'Israeli assassinations have eliminated more than half of Iran's most senior leadership.' Larijani's killing is among the most significant post-Khamenei leadership losses Iran has suffered.

title: USS Gerald R. Ford Sustains 30-Hour Onboard Fire — Heading for Repairs
detail: The USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78), the primary US carrier strike group in the Eastern Mediterranean, sustained a serious 30-hour onboard fire and has been ordered to depart the theater for emergency repairs at a NATO facility. The cause of the fire has not been officially confirmed — the Navy cited an 'internal mechanical incident' while Iran's media claimed it was struck by an IRGC missile. The Ford's departure temporarily reduces US carrier aviation capability in the theater to a single carrier (USS Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea) until the USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) completes its pre-deployment certification. No US sailor deaths from the fire were confirmed by the Navy.

title: IDF Strikes IRGC Security HQ, Logistics Center, and Ballistic Missile Command
detail: The IDF confirmed strikes on three high-priority IRGC targets on Day 19: the IRGC Security Unit headquarters; the IRGC General Logistics and Support Department maintenance center; and a ballistic missile array command center. US forces independently struck hardened Iranian missile sites near the Strait of Hormuz with 5,000-pound GBU-28/57-type deep-penetrating bunker buster munitions. Netanyahu addressed the nation, declaring 'The IDF is watching you from the skies of Iran,' and sent Nowruz (Persian New Year) wishes to the Iranian people — a calculated message timed to coincide with the March 20 Nowruz holiday to maximize domestic pressure on the Iranian regime.

title: Trump Counterterrorism Chief Joe Kent Resigns Over Iran War — Highest-Level Departure
detail: Joe Kent, Trump's National Counterterrorism Center director, resigned his position — becoming the highest-ranking US official to leave the administration in protest over the Iran war decision. Kent, a decorated Army Special Forces veteran and Trump loyalist, reportedly told colleagues he was unable to defend the strategic rationale for Operation Epic Fury. His resignation is the most significant indication of internal dissent within the Trump national security apparatus. He joins a growing list of military and intelligence officials who have privately questioned the war's objectives and timeline.

title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Nearly Doubles as Iran Allows More Vessels — Possible Tactical Concession
detail: Commercial shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz nearly doubled from previous conflict lows, suggesting Iran may be allowing more vessels to transit as a tactical concession to reduce economic pressure on itself and Gulf neighbors. Iraq is actively negotiating with Iran to allow Iraqi oil tankers through the strait. The US previously struck hardened Iranian missile sites near the Hormuz with deep penetrators to reduce IRGC capacity to threaten commercial shipping. Analysts caution the opening may be temporary and partial. Oil prices responded, with Brent falling to ~$100.90 and WTI to ~$92.47 — the first sustained decline since the conflict began.

title: Hezbollah Fires Dozens of Rockets; IDF Issues Tyre Evacuation Warning; Beirut Bachoura Struck
detail: Hezbollah fired dozens of rockets at northern Israel overnight, triggering IDF counter-strikes on Hezbollah rocket launcher positions across southern Lebanon. The IDF issued formal evacuation warnings for the Tyre area of southern Lebanon ahead of a larger anticipated Israeli ground and air operation. An Israeli airstrike hit the Bachoura neighborhood of central Beirut — a densely populated area. Lebanese Civil Defense reported 11 members wounded in an IDF raid on Bekaa Valley infrastructure. IDF ground forces continue pushing deeper into southern Lebanon. US diplomats are reportedly encouraging Syria to assist in disarming Hezbollah as part of a potential Lebanon-separate ceasefire track.

title: Israeli Reports: Mojtaba Khamenei Wounded, Mother/Wife/Son Reportedly Killed
detail: Israeli intelligence reports indicate Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's Supreme Leader appointed March 8, was wounded in an Israeli airstrike and that his mother, wife, and son were killed. These reports have not been independently verified and Iran has not confirmed. No photo or video of Mojtaba Khamenei has emerged since he was named Supreme Leader. Iranian state media insists he is 'leading' the defense. US intelligence officially assesses his status as 'unknown but degraded.' Trump said on March 17 it was 'unclear if Mojtaba Khamenei is alive or not.' US intelligence separately assessed that the Iranian regime will remain structurally intact even if Mojtaba Khamenei is killed or incapacitated.

title: Arab and Islamic Foreign Ministers Convene in Riyadh on Regional Security
detail: Arab and Islamic foreign ministers convened an emergency meeting in Riyadh to coordinate a response to the expanding Iran-US/Israel conflict. The meeting brings together Gulf state FMs, OIC members, and key regional actors to develop a unified diplomatic position. Qatar warned Gulf states will face Iranian retaliation if the US ends the war unilaterally without securing Iran's agreement. Russia announced it is expanding military cooperation with Iran. EU foreign policy chief confirmed the bloc is working with Middle East partners on conflict resolution. Iran executed Kouroush Keyvani, convicted of espionage for Israel, via its judiciary, with Iran's Mizan News Agency confirming the hanging.
